There is no\n\"moral victory\" clause: frontier parity is a FAIL of W1, however interesting the\narchitecture.\n\n---\n\n## 2. The measurement + fair-comparison framework (where the programme lives or dies)\n\nComparing a neurosymbolic system against the neural/RAG frontier at a fixed\nresource budget is the single hardest methodological problem here — harder than\nany individual architecture. The framework is frozen as **P3-MF (KOT-FAIR/2)**\nbefore any Phase-2 W1-relevant experiment freezes, and it now begins with an\nexplicit threat model.\n\n### 2.0 The threat model (new; P3-D-THREAT)\n\n- DECISION: KOT-FAIR/2 includes a formal **resource/baseline threat model**: an\n  enumerated adversary analysis of how each metric can be gamed by either side —\n  packaging games (padding, base-image migration, remote dependencies), band/\n  frontier games (comparator dodging, budget shopping), cost games (warm-cache-only\n  reporting, output-length inflation/deflation, batch-size shopping,\n  symbolic-work-hiding), index games (suite shopping, abstention gaming, domain\n  reweighting), store games (answer-key encoding, error-analysis-driven authoring,\n  researcher adaptation to the frozen suite), and tuning games (unequal search\n  compute behind equal config counts) — each with its named mechanised counter in\n  this section. The threat model is a frozen artifact; a game not counterable at\n  freeze time is documented as a standing limitation on every claim it touches\n  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; answers review §4 \"formal resource/baseline threat\n  model\"].\n\n### 2.1 Confound A — size/packaging asymmetry\n\nAnswered by KOT-SIZE/2 (§1.2): canonical packing script, six co-reported figures,\nfrozen base image, remote-dependency rule, artifact-freeze (store hash pinned at\neval time, so \"grow the store after measuring\" is impossible by construction), and\npeak-RSS/cold-start measurement so lazily-paged stores pay their true cost on the\nresource vector.\n\n### 2.2 Confound B — the answer-key problem (the deepest one)\n\nA store can encode benchmark answers far more directly than weights can. The f2b\nlesson is exactly this in miniature:\n\n- PREMISE: the f2b verifier accepts iff the answer string-equals the canonical\n  record while gold is DEFINED by that same equality, so the measured +0.1507 is\n  provably consistent with \"aligned answer key + retry\", and the shuffled control\n  cannot discriminate content from alignment [MEASURED:\n  registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license].\n\nMechanised answers, all mandatory for any W1 claim [STIPULATED: ASM-0812]:\n\n1. **Decontamination audit (hard gate, win-voiding):** automated n-gram overlap +\n   embedding-similarity screen of every kernel/world/store record against every\n   item of the frozen index suite, pinned threshold, human spot-check protocol on\n   flagged pairs; stores may not be authored, selected, or grown using any index\n   item, its paraphrase, or its source page. A win whose store fails the audit is\n   VOID, not caveated. (Neural baselines carry the standard training-data\n   contamination caveat symmetrically: we report known contamination status of\n   baseline corpora but cannot audit closed corpora — an asymmetry that favours\n   the baselines' scores, i.e. conservative for us.)\n2. **The factorial control design** (replacing revision 1's derangement-centric\n   list — derangement alone changes alignment and retrieval behaviour\n   simultaneously and cannot localise what mattered): controls span\n   **content-type × retrieval-architecture × executor**, drawn per experiment from:\n   (a) seed-pinned deranged store→item addressing (the f2b control, inherited);\n   (b) label permutation within records; (c) irrelevant-but-structurally-matched\n   records (same schema, types, sizes; unrelated content); (d) edge/relation\n   shuffling for graph arms; (e) the aligned-non-kernel store (matched-size,\n   matched-alignment, plain-English typed records — the knull lesson); (f) the\n   **matched generic-RAG/tool-use control** (§2.3, the strongest missing control);\n   (g) kernel-as-text null at matched token budget (the null Programme-1/2 never\n   lost to and that nsk1-g2d showed can be actively harmful — stays mandatory).\n   Each Phase-2 prereg names which cells it instantiates and what each\n   discriminates [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; answers review \"Use a factorial control\n   design\"].\n\n### 2.2a The sealed evaluation (new; P3-D-SEAL)\n\nDecontamination screening is necessary but CANNOT certify fairness: it misses\nbenchmark-source knowledge encoded as rules/abstractions, selective store\nauthoring guided by benchmark error analysis, paraphrases below threshold,\ncontamination already in donor weights, and repeated researcher adaptation to the\nfrozen suite (the review's list, adopted verbatim) [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].\n\n- DECISION: every W1 claim additionally requires a **sealed evaluation**: a\n  secondary suite produced AFTER architecture + store freeze, by an independent\n  party or a pinned procedural generator, unseen by every developing agent;\n  for store-based systems it includes temporally-fresh facts and/or independently\n  held-out domains. The sealed suite is versioned per release (LiveBench-style\n  refresh discipline — each release a new version, never silently folded in). Gate\n  shape: the sealed-suite result must be directionally consistent with the frozen\n  suite (pre-registered consistency band); a large frozen-vs-sealed gap voids the\n  win pending diagnosis [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].\n\n### 2.3 Confound C — baseline strength, the frontier-builder, and tuning symmetry\n\n- DECISION: the comparator set F(B_k) is built by a standing **neural\n  frontier-builder** (P3-D-FRONTIER): for each pre-registered budget B_k it\n  produces the strongest baselines that fit, drawing on (i) the strongest open\n  pure-neural models at or below budget (pinned name+revision at freeze);\n  (ii) budget-optimising transforms — quantisation, structured pruning,\n  distillation — applied best-effort to those models; (iii) **neural + retrieval:\n  a conventional RAG / tool-use architecture over the SAME corpus, SAME\n  retriever/index, SAME budgets as the kernel arm** (the strongest missing control\n  — the aligned-non-kernel store does NOT replace it); (iv) optimised/adaptive\n  test-time compute — best-of-k self-consistency alone is too weak; the builder\n  implements compute-optimal adaptive strategies (REVIEW-CITED: the ICLR-2025\n  test-time-compute result; verified by P3-LR-RAG/P3-LR-EVAL) and learned/neural\n  verifiers where budget permits; (v) training-compute-matched from-scratch twins,\n  required only where the neurosymbolic arm itself trains from scratch (H-GU)\n  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].\n- DECISION: the six-way attribution factorisation is a standing analysis\n  requirement: any observed win must be decomposed as far as the control cells\n  allow across **kernel semantics vs structured storage vs retrieval vs\n  deterministic execution vs retry/search vs neural-symbolic integration** — the\n  factorial cells in §2.2(2) + the RAG control are exactly the instruments for\n  this [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].\n- DECISION: tuning symmetry binds on **total tuning compute** (accelerator-hours +\n  CPU-hours actually spent on search, logged per arm into KOT-LIFE/1), with a\n  FIXED, pre-registered selection rule on a dev split disjoint from the index\n  suite — equal config counts are NOT sufficient (configs differ enormously in\n  cost). An architecture that only wins with more search compute has not won\n  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; corrects revision 1's config-count rule].\n\n### 2.4 What P3-MF-0 must deliver (the framework as an artifact)\n\nA frozen `KOT-FAIR/2` spec: the threat model (§2.0); index suite pins + domain\nstructure + normalisation constants (§1.1); the KOT-SIZE/2 canonical packing\nscript + six-figure recipe + frozen base image hash; the KOT-COST/2 measurement\nrig + the P3-D-HW hardware-repeatability and warm/cold protocol; the KOT-LIFE/1\nledger template; the decontamination screener; the sealed-eval production\nprotocol; the frontier-builder spec + comparator pinning rule; the statistical\nanalysis plan (§2.5); and a **calibration report**: the full framework run on\npure-neural models ONLY (135M vs 360M vs 1.7B), demonstrating that the index\nseparates known-ordered models with the expected ordering and stable variance,\nand that the cost rig reproduces across days/reboots within its pre-registered\nrepeatability band — the instrument is validated on cases with known answers\nbefore it ever judges a neurosymbolic system. Phase-0/1 work on the local box +\nfree credits, ~$0.\n\n\n### Baselines need strengthening\n\nThe strongest missing control is:\n\n> Same corpus, same retriever/index, same resource budgets, but a conventional neural RAG or tool-use architecture.\n\nThe aligned-non-kernel store does not replace this. The programme must distinguish:\n\n- kernel semantics;\n- structured storage;\n- retrieval;\n- deterministic execution;\n- retry/search;\n- specialized neural-symbolic integration.\n\nBaseline optimization should include quantization, structured pruning, distillation, retrieval, and compute-optimal sampling or verification. Best-of-\\(k\\) self-consistency alone is too weak: adaptive test-time compute can substantially outperform naive best-of-\\(N\\). [ICLR 2025 test-time-compute study](https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/1b623663fd9b874366f3ce019fdfdd44-Abstract-Conference.html).\n\nEqual numbers of hyperparameter configurations are also not sufficient symmetry because configurations differ greatly in cost. Match total tuning compute and use fixed selection rules.\n\n### Decontamination is necessary but cannot certify fairness\n\nN-gram and embedding overlap screening can catch gross answer-key leakage, but not:\n\n- benchmark source knowledge encoded as rules or abstractions;\n- selective store authoring based on benchmark error analysis;\n- paraphrases below the threshold;\n- benchmark contamination in donor weights;\n- repeated researcher adaptation to the frozen test suite.\n\nAdd a sealed evaluation component produced after architecture and store freeze, preferably by an independent party or procedural generator. For store-based systems, include temporally fresh facts or independently held-out domains.\n\nUse a factorial control design: content type × retrieval architecture × executor. Derangement alone is weak because it changes alignment and often retrieval behavior simultaneously. Include label permutation, irrelevant-but-structurally-matched records, edge shuffling, and matched generic RAG.\n\n### Statistical specification needs correction\n\nW1 should require the lower confidence bound on the difference to exceed the practical margin:\n\n\\[\nLCB_{95}(INDEX(S)-INDEX(B)) > \\delta_k\n\\]\n\nA point estimate above \\(\\delta_k\\) plus an LCB merely above zero does not establish the margin.\n\nBecause S is tested against multiple baselines, use simultaneous confidence bounds or family-wise error control. Bootstrap hierarchically across benchmark families and items, preserving paired predictions.\n\n“TOST non-inferiority” is technically incorrect. TOST is normally an equivalence procedure with two one-sided tests. A non-inferiority claim uses the appropriate single one-sided test or confidence bound against the non-inferiority margin.\n\n## The AI index\n\nThe current nine-task suite is reasonable for SmolLM2 harness calibration. It is not adequate as the headline “AI-capability index.”\n\nSeven components are closely related multiple-choice commonsense or factual tasks. An unweighted macro-average therefore overweights that construct while underrepresenting:\n\n- language modeling and linguistic competence;\n- instruction following;\n- natural-language generation;\n- compositional and relational generalization;\n- logical reasoning;\n- calibration and selective prediction;\n- multilingual performance;\n- coding below R-3;\n- robustness under distribution shift.\n\nThe raw average also combines binary, four-way, five-way, and open-ended tasks with different chance floors and variances. Normalize scores relative to chance and a pinned ceiling, then macro-average first within capability domains and only then across domains. Publish both the domain vector and the scalar.\n\nMMLU-cloze is particularly problematic: the repository itself notes inconsistent MMLU variants across SmolLM2 sizes. It should not be silently treated as a stable cross-rung component.\n\nRecommended structure:\n\n- **R-0:** held-out language-model loss plus BabyLM-style BLiMP/EWoK linguistic evaluation; small procedural relational and rule-reasoning suites.\n- **R-1/R-2 general capability:** a reduced set from HellaSwag, ARC-Challenge, PIQA, WinoGrande, MMLU, GSM8K, plus instruction following where above floor.\n- **Neurosymbolic diagnostics:** CLUTRR, ProofWriter/RuleTaker, FOLIO or similar, with held-out rules, relation compositions, proof depths, and lexical paraphrases. CLUTRR explicitly evaluates systematic relational generalization and includes natural-language graph construction, making it especially relevant. [CLUTRR paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.06177).\n- **R-3+:** MMLU-Pro, BBH or BBEH where discriminative, IFEval, code, and a frozen release of LiveBench or another recent objective benchmark.\n- **Contamination-resistant secondary:** a sealed or periodically refreshed suite. LiveBench is useful because it refreshes questions and uses objective scoring, though its changing nature means each release must be versioned rather than folded silently into a fixed index. [LiveBench](https://github.com/livebench/livebench).\n\nHELM’s multi-scenario, multi-metric structure is a better conceptual model than a flat accuracy average. [Stanford HELM](https://nlp.stanford.edu/helm/vhelm/). The Open LLM Leaderboard v2 suite is suitable at larger scales but the document is right that much of it may floor out at tiny scales. [Official benchmark descriptions](https://huggingface.co/docs/leaderboards/main/open_llm_leaderboard/about).\n\nKeep the current suite as “SmolLM2 continuity index,” not as the sole capability index.\n\n## 2. Architecture bets\n\n### Verifier loop\n\nThis is the best-motivated family because it has the only measured system-level positive shape. The proposed index-level falsifier is sensible, provided it compares against:\n\n- compute-optimal neural sampling;\n- a learned neural verifier;\n- generic structured RAG;\n- grammar/constrained decoding;\n- the same verifier with a non-kernel store.\n\nRun a coverage upper-bound calculation first:\n\n\\[\n\\Delta_{\\text{maximum index}} \\leq \\sum_b w_b \\kappa_b\n(1-\\text{baseline accuracy on covered items})\n\\]\n\nIf even perfect covered-item correction cannot reach \\(\\delta\\), kill the index-mover claim without GPU work.\n\n### GNN fusion\n\nThe hypothesis is plausible, particularly for relational composition and systematic generalization. But “GNN ≤ text serialization” at R-0/R-1 does not kill the entire family. Text is often superior for tiny graphs, while graph encoders may become useful only as graph size and reasoning depth grow.\n\nControls should include:\n\n- same retrieved nodes with no edges;\n- shuffled edges and relation labels;\n- MLP/DeepSets/Transformer encoders with the same inputs;\n- text serialization at equal information and resource budgets;\n- held-out relation compositions and proof depths;\n- oracle graph versus NL-extracted graph.\n\nSeparate four stages: retrieval, graph construction, graph encoding, and LM use. A positive delivery probe is insufficient; require causal behavioral improvement.\n\n### Rule placement\n\nThe cheap-first order CD → KV → adapters/activations → hidden layer is sensible. Several claims need tightening:\n\n- Constrained decoding does not itself perform inference; it enforces an output language unless the external engine has already derived the valid continuation set.\n- Attention weights are not adequate provenance evidence. KV provenance requires causal ablation, intervention, or executor traces.\n- A dedicated concept bottleneck is not strongly interpretable merely because its units are named. Provenance must be causally validated.\n- Cost parity with the external loop should not be the only endpoint. An internal mechanism might be worthwhile if it improves accuracy, coverage, or latency variance. Compare Pareto frontiers.\n\nA promising missing design is explicit NL-to-program synthesis: parse into a small executable DSL, execute deterministically, and generate from the checked result with calibrated abstention. That attacks the actual boundary problem more directly than hoping rule knowledge emerges in a hidden layer.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
poc/gpt56-review/rev-lrrag-20260711/events.jsonl:29:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"agent_message","text":"Verdict: not sound to commit as P3-LR-RAG yet. The bibliography is largely real and the review has the right fairness instincts, but it overstates how much Phase-1 design uncertainty it closes.\n\n## Three most important concerns\n\n1. **P3-D-RAGC is not actually de-risked.**\n\n   The hand-off calls the control checklist “closed,” but it never specifies an executable matched control. “Same source snapshot” is not the same as “same corpus, same retriever/index”: converting source material into kernel records, plain-text passages, triples, or tool schemas changes the indexed objects and retrieval problem. The design still needs explicit rules for:\n\n   - equivalent source-content derivation and information parity;\n   - identical versus architecture-specific retrievers;\n   - retrieved-token/context budgets and ordering;\n   - generator, tool executor, retry policy, and tuning-compute parity;\n   - store/index construction leakage and authoring effort.\n\n   KILT supports a common Wikipedia snapshot and provenance scoring, but it does not establish the proposed cross-representation “same retriever/index” control ([KILT](https://arxiv.org/abs/2009.02252)). Consequently, [RAG.md §8](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/RAG.md:322) claims substantially more closure than the literature provides.\n\n2. **The graph evidence does not support the GNN hand-off or the asserted break-even threshold.**\n\n   The sources concern GraphRAG retrieval, graph-based indexing, PageRank, and global summarisation—not GNN-to-LM fusion versus text serialisation. There is no GNN-fusion paper in the ledger. HippoRAG shows a graph retriever can beat iterative retrieval on selected multi-hop QA; MiniRAG reports comparable SLM performance and 25% storage; neither establishes that “structure wins only past a hops/aggregation threshold,” that gains increase monotonically with depth, or that a GNN encoder becomes preferable.\n\n   MiniRAG’s causal interpretation—graph structure “substitutes for” weak SLM semantics—is stronger than its reported system comparison ([MiniRAG](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.06713)). The review correctly admits that no size×depth sweep exists, then treats the hypothesised threshold as settled in its hand-off. P3-D-GNN therefore remains dependent on P3-LR-FUSE and an explicit “no prior” statement; RAG.md cannot close its empirical premise.\n\n3. **P3-D-FRONTIER is under-specified despite the ICLR result being cited correctly.**\n\n   The Snell et al. headline is accurate: over 4× efficiency versus best-of-N and a conditional 14×-larger-model comparison on MATH ([ICLR 2025 paper](https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/1b623663fd9b874366f3ce019fdfdd44-Paper-Conference.pdf)). The missing constraints are important:\n\n   - experiments used PaLM-2 models, math, fine-tuned revision models and PRMs—not 135M–1.7B general models;\n   - prompt-difficulty estimation itself consumes non-trivial test-time compute;\n   - the 4× and 14× findings are separate comparisons, not a general “4× compute buys one model class” exchange rate;\n   - the authors explicitly leave generalisation beyond math/easily verified domains unresolved;\n   - the 14× result depends on difficulty and the assumed lifetime inference/pretraining ratio.\n\n   Thus “implement Snell-style” is not yet a baseline recipe. P3-D-FRONTIER still needs a budgeted difficulty estimator, eligible search policies by rung, verifier training/build costs, fallback when estimated base success is near zero, and a non-math calibration gate.\n\n## Source and claim audit\n\nI found no clearly hallucinated paper among the 28 ledger entries. Most titles, venues and headline results are real. The ledger nevertheless contains errors and overstatements:\n\n- **Source count is wrong:** the JSONL contains 28 entries—27 `verified:true`, one `false`—not “27 sources; 26 true, one false.”\n- **FlashRAG counts are wrong or unpinned:** RAG.md says 16 methods and 38 datasets; the primary abstract reports 12 methods and 32 datasets ([FlashRAG](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13576)). If these are later toolkit counts, cite and pin that release rather than attributing them to the paper.\n- **RETRO accounting overreach:** 2T tokens and 25× fewer parameters are correct ([RETRO](https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.04426)); “under a bytes-inclusive ledger the comparison reverses sign” is an unperformed calculation, not a measured result.\n- **Universal accounting claims are unsupported:** “no published small-model RAG win would survive” and “stricter than anything in this literature” require a systematic search plus actual byte/build/query calculations. The current 28-paper sweep does not establish either.\n- **GraphRAG cost statement is too absolute:** the flagship paper does report construction/runtime information, including indexing wall time for its corpus, even if it lacks the full lifecycle ledger sought here. Say “no complete bytes/build-compute ledger,” not “cost not reported.”\n- **KILT is mischaracterised as precedent for same-retriever controls.** It pins the knowledge snapshot and evaluates provenance; it does not mandate identical retrievers across systems.\n- **TinyAgent’s result is accurately reported but not resource matched:** its 80.06% versus 79.08% comparison is on a 16-tool, task-specific Mac-assistant suite and includes a separately fine-tuned DeBERTa tool selector ([TinyAgent](https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-demo.9/)). That selector’s weights, training and inference belong in the system ledger.\n- **APIGen is less “narrow-domain” than stated:** it remains function-calling-specific, but spans 3,673 APIs across 21 categories; grouping it with TinyAgent as uniformly narrow curated-domain evidence is too coarse ([APIGen](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.18518)).\n- **MiniRAG’s “25% storage” is an author-reported relative comparison**, not evidence that the complete system fits 25% of an honestly packed LLM-RAG deployment budget.\n- **The large-budget verifier failure is not universal.** The cited Scaling Flaws result is important evidence for particular learned verifiers/search procedures; it does not establish that every learned-verifier frontier must degrade, nor that the symbolic system is immune end-to-end.\n\n## Assessment by topic\n\n- **Small-model RAG/tool use:** useful examples and appropriately skeptical of parameter-only wins, but not a defensible “strongest current” survey or frontier recipe. It lacks a documented search protocol and important negative/generalisation evidence.\n- **Structured versus dense retrieval:** the high-level judgement—dense/lexical retrieval is strong for local fact lookup, graphs can help relational/global tasks, neither dominates—is reasonable and supported by the cited GraphRAG comparisons. The claimed hop/depth break-even is not supported.\n- **Index accounting:** the proposed accounting policy is good. The audit of what individual papers actually count is too qualitative to justify its universal conclusions.\n- **Unmatched-compute wins:** most are correctly caveated, but RETRO’s alleged reversed result, MiniRAG’s causal/storage reading, and the Snell synthesis still convert unmatched findings into stronger conclusions than warranted.\n- **Adaptive test-time compute:** primary result verified, envelope partly captured, implementation and transfer costs insufficiently handled.\n- **P3-D-RAGC / FRONTIER / GNN:** useful input to all three, but none is genuinely closed. RAGC is closest; FRONTIER needs a calibration/budget protocol; GNN is not de-risked by this source set.\n\nRecommendation: revise before committing as P3-LR-RAG. Downgrade “settled/closed” language, correct the factual errors, make the RAGC control operational, and explicitly leave GNN fusion to P3-LR-FUSE."}}

SQ2 — NL→DSL / program synthesis WITH execution feedback\n\n(Decoder-side grammar machinery is owned by the prior review; here: what the executor\nbuys, and its precise limit.)\n\n- **Execution-guided decoding** (condition decoding on execution of partial programs):\n  universally improves 4 architectures on WikiSQL/ATIS/GeoQuery; 83.8% EX WikiSQL in 2018\n  [LIT: egd-2018]. What Programme-3 is, and what it inherits\n\nThe maintainer's intent, restated: use something *like* the kernel + world\ninformation this programme has built to create **neurosymbolic AI architectures**\nthat (1) achieve a **higher AI index than comparably-resourced models**, and then\n(2) find how **small** the whole system (model + kernel + world + everything else)\ncan be made while staying competitive on that index. Because the system is\nneurosymbolic, size cannot be parameters: size is **deployment bytes** plus a full\n**inference resource vector**. Start very small to learn cheaply which\narchitectures work; then continuously scale while improving them. (The review is\nright that the literal \"higher than ALL other models of the same size and compute\"\nis not a testable claim; §1.4 narrows what W1 actually licenses — the maintainer's\nnorth-star is preserved as the *aspiration*, the testable claim is the frontier\nstatement.)\n\nProgramme-3 is a **new goal**, not a new evidence base. It inherits Programme-1/2's\ninstruments, seams, and — critically — its measured walls. The honest starting\nposition, restated from the capstone with its own tags:\n\n- PREMISE: across the entire registry there are ZERO audited end-task wins over the\n  kernel-as-text null attributable to the kernel's content; registry/assessments/b-cov-define-lane.json].\n- PREMISE: model-internal grounding is unresolved two-sided: text-delivered\n  grounding measured NET-HARMFUL (0.76 → 0.43 on append; 0/24 rescues), the\n  residual-stream channel delivers at echo grade (keyacc 0.81/0.85) but integration\n  into free generation is unresolved (R− rescue 0/8) [MEASURED:\n  registry/assessments/nsk1-g2d.json + nsk1-bprime2.json, exploratory/DRAFT status].\n- PREMISE: cross-lingual concept minting shows a large MEMBERSHIP UPPER BOUND on\n  prefill savings (~18.5–24.0% Qwen2.5 / 33.4–41.7% SmolLM2 at the 10k-concept\n  anchor) with the entire consumption channel and mint-cost side unmeasured\n  [MEASURED: registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json, Tier-0 exploratory census — an\n  upper bound, never achievable savings].\n\nThree consequences shape everything below.\n\n- DECISION: Programme-3 adopts the four inherited hard constraints — (1) no\n  real-input claim without an FK-NLB-3-class NL front-end gate cleared per vertical;\n  (2) every kernel-contribution claim carries deranged-store + aligned-non-kernel\n  controls; (3) coverage numbers stay corpus/rung/kernel-state-indexed; (4)\n  membership/compression figures are upper bounds until a consumption channel is\n  measured [STIPULATED: ASM-0808].\n- DECISION: constraint (1) is strengthened from a claims rule to a DEPENDENCY rule —\n  every experiment whose store is addressed from natural input (verifier-loop NL\n  legs, GNN retrieval from NL queries, KV rule-firing from NL, constrained decoding\n  over NL prompts, H-DD store reinjection on NL tasks) is BLOCKED by the NLB gate\n  (P3-E-NLB-1 clearing 0.90 retention + S2 fail-closed per vertical), OR runs on\n  ORACLE inputs (gold parses/subgraphs/record addresses) and is labelled\n  `oracle-diagnostic` in its prereg, licensing NO W1 claim and no real-input claim\n  of any kind [STIPULATED: ASM-0814; the review's ranked concern #3 verbatim].\n- DECISION: Programme-3's headline claims are **system-level competitive claims**\n  (index at a pre-registered resource budget), not kernel-content-attribution\n  claims — this is a deliberate re-aiming: even if a win turns out to be \"aligned\n  typed store + checker\" rather than \"NSM semantics\", it still satisfies the\n  maintainer's north-star, and the attribution question is carried as a separate,\n  honest, secondary analysis rather than a blocker [STIPULATED: ASM-0800].\n\nRelation to the existing L0–L4 ladder (`docs/next/architecture-ladder.md`): the\nladder enumerated *where the kernel can touch a model*; Programme-3 reuses those\nseams as components but changes the objective function from \"make the kernel more\ncentral\" to \"beat the resource-constrained baseline frontier on a pinned index,\nthen shrink\". Rules-placement options in §3.3 map onto L2a/L2b/L3c; the GNN fusion\nfamily extends the A2/E5 trained-bridge seam; the verifier-loop system is F2b\nproductised; the program-synthesis family (H-PS, new in this revision) is the\ndirect attack on the measured NL boundary.\n\n---\n\n## 1. North-star and falsifiable success criteria\n\n### 1.1 The AI index — KOT-AI-INDEX/2 (domain-structured), plus the continuity index\n\nThe review's diagnosis is accepted in full: revision 1's nine-task suite is seven\ncorrelated multiple-choice commonsense/factual tasks plus two others, raw-averaged\nacross different chance floors and variances — adequate as a *SmolLM2 harness\ncontinuity* instrument, inadequate as a headline capability index [STIPULATED:\nASM-0811, answering review ranked concern #2].\n\n- DECISION: the headline \"AI index\" is **KOT-AI-INDEX/2**, constructed as follows.\n  (1) Every per-benchmark score is normalised against its chance floor and a pinned\n  ceiling: s̃ = (s − chance)/(ceiling − chance), with the ceiling pinned at index\n  freeze (published human/large-reference performance where it exists, else a named\n  pinned reference model's own-harness score). (2) Normalised scores are\n  macro-averaged first WITHIN capability domains, then across domains, HELM-style\n  (multi-scenario, multi-metric — REVIEW-CITED: Stanford HELM; verified by\n  P3-LR-EVAL before freeze). (3) Every publication reports the **domain vector AND\n  the scalar**; the scalar exists for ranking, the vector for honesty — no\n  Programme-3 claim may quote the scalar without the vector one link away.\n  (4) Suite membership, domain assignment, normalisation constants, harness\n  version, prompts, and scoring are all pinned in the P3-D-INDEX freeze; any change\n  is an index VERSION change that re-baselines everything [STIPULATED: ASM-0811].\n\nProvisional domain structure (final membership fixed by P3-D-INDEX after\nP3-LR-EVAL verifies floors/ceilings at each rung; the review's ranked concern #3 verbatim].\n- DECISION: Programme-3's headline claims are **system-level competitive claims**\n  (index at a pre-registered resource budget), not kernel-content-attribution\n  claims — this is a deliberate re-aiming: even if a win turns out to be \"aligned\n  typed store + checker\" rather than \"NSM semantics\", it still satisfies the\n  maintainer's north-star, and the attribution question is carried as a separate,\n  honest, secondary analysis rather than a blocker [STIPULATED: ASM-0800].\n\nRelation to the existing L0–L4 ladder (`docs/next/architecture-ladder.md`): the\nladder enumerated *where the kernel can touch a model*; Programme-3 reuses those\nseams as components but changes the objective function from \"make the kernel more\ncentral\" to \"beat the resource-constrained baseline frontier on a pinned index,\nthen shrink\". Rules-placement options in §3.3 map onto L2a/L2b/L3c; the GNN fusion\nfamily extends the A2/E5 trained-bridge seam; the verifier-loop system is F2b\nproductised; the program-synthesis family (H-PS, new in this revision) is the\nDesign ruling from the analysis (a prior ordering, not a verdict):\n\n- DECISION: Phase-2 tests the placements in the order CD → KV → {AD, ACT} → HL —\n  cheapest, least invasive, most provenance-preserving, most fail-closed first;\n  HL is deferred until a shallower placement has shown a positive, because every\n  deep-internal channel measured so far is unresolved-at-best while the\n  external/decode-side seam holds the programme's only positive [STIPULATED:\n  ASM-0802; the supporting facts are §0's nsk1 and f2b premises].\n- **Endpoint, corrected:** cost parity with the external loop is NOT the sole\n  endpoint — an internal placement can be worthwhile via accuracy, coverage, or\n  latency-variance improvements even at equal cost. The family head-to-head\n  (H-RULE-CD/KV vs H-VL at R-1) is decided on **Pareto frontiers over (accuracy on\n  the covered slice, coverage reached, p95 latency, resource vector)**, with the\n  pre-registered primary being frontier dominance, not a single-axis comparison\n  [STIPULATED: ASM-0815; answers review \"Compare Pareto frontiers\"]. Registered\n  projection: ASM-0806.\n\n### 3.3a H-PS — NL→program synthesis (new; first-class)\n\nThe review's \"promising missing design\", adopted as a family rather than a\nfootnote because it attacks the programme's actual measured wall (the NL\nboundary) directly instead of hoping rule knowledge emerges in a hidden layer\n[STIPULATED: ASM-0815]:\n\n- **Design shape:** parse natural input into a small executable DSL (the kernel's\n  closed grammar, or a thin typed layer over it); execute deterministically\n  (the µs engine); generate the surface answer FROM the checked result; abstain\n  with calibration when parse confidence is low (selective prediction — the\n  parser emits a calibrated confidence and the system's abstention policy is part\n  of the measured product).\n- Evidence status: cautioned — this is exactly the l3a-parse/a5-nl path, both\n  measured FAIL at scope with the a1-hybrid front-end; H-PS is the *redesign* of\n  that path with modern semantic-parsing/program-synthesis methods and calibrated\n  abstention, which is why P3-D-PS and P3-D-NLB are both blocked by the\n  P3-LR-PARSE lit-review (do not repeat the measured parser failure blindly).\n- Relation to P3-D-NLB: NLB is the shared *gate instrument* (retention + S2\n  fail-closed measurement per vertical); H-PS is an *architecture family* whose\n  front-end, if it clears NLB, becomes the shared front-end other families use.\n  They co-design; the gate stays independent of any one family's success.\n- Cheapest falsifier: G2/G3 on the two existing verticals — oracle-parse ceiling\n  (known: engine exact) vs synthesised-parse retention, against the pinned 0.90\n  retention + S2 bars; then the D4 diagnostics with held-out compositions.\n\n### 3.4 H-RB — retrieve-vs-bake (the store-placement fork, cross-cutting)\n\nFor any store-using family: store on disk + retrieval at inference (bytes cheap,\nper-query resource cost) vs distilled/baked into weights (bytes expensive, zero\nper-query retrieval). A pure size↔cost trade under KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2,\nswept, not hypothesised: each Phase-2 winner is measured at both settings and the\nPareto points reported. (The a-e2 dense-I/O membership bound — an upper bound, §0\n— stays quarantined until its consumption channel is measured.)\n\n### 3.5 The build-strategy fork: H-GU vs H-DD (compare cheaply at small scale)\n\n**H-GU — ground-up neurosymbolic training.** Train small hosts from scratch with\nthe symbolic interface present from step 0. Only at rungs R-0/R-1 initially\nat source or flagged, each prior failure judged capability-limited vs\nfundamental, and the named design beads spawned with their opening questions\nanswered.\n\n| Honest risks and kill criteria\n\n### 7.1 Programme-level kill — K-P3v2 (quantitative, hardened)\n\nRevision 1's kill contained a subjective escape hatch (\"analysts attribute the\nmisses to fundamentals\") and a soft default (open-ended maintainer fork). Both\nare replaced [STIPULATED: ASM-0818; answers review \"The programme-level kill is\ntoo soft\"].\n\n- DECISION: **K-P3v2 (verbatim)** [STIPULATED: ASM-0818]. The competitiveness\n  programme is KILLED — not forked, not re-scoped in place — if ANY of the\n  following pre-registered quantitative conditions holds at its named checkpoint:\n  1. **Coverage ceiling:** after the P3-D-POWER analysis (including any funded\n     coverage-growth ingestion wave completed by the R-1 checkpoint), the\n     perfect-oracle bound Δ_max < δ_1 for EVERY store-dependent family on the\n     frozen R-1 suite — perfect use of the store cannot reach the margin, so no\n     mechanism result can save it.\n  2. **NL boundary:** P3-E-NLB-1 (and its redesigns) fails the 0.90 retention OR\n     the S2 fail-closed safety gate on BOTH verticals after **N = 2 pre-registered\n     redesign cycles** beyond the first attempt.\n  3. **Frontier:** no family's G4 result exceeds the neural/RAG frontier F(B_k)\n     at BOTH R-1 and R-2 (i.e. W1 fails everywhere through R-2 with the frontier\n     comparators properly built).\n  4. **Attribution collapse:** every measured family gain vanishes against the\n     matched generic-store / generic-RAG controls (the win is retrieval + typed\n     storage, available to any baseline, and is absorbed into the frontier).\n  5. **Boundary non-survival:** every family's G2 (oracle-interface) gain fails\n     to survive G3 (natural input) at the pre-registered retention margin, across\n     the N = 2 NLB redesign cycles.\n  6. **Lifecycle domination:** the KOT-LIFE/1 ledger shows S's total cost of\n     ownership dominated by a frontier baseline at ALL THREE pinned amortisation\n     volumes (10^3/10^6/10^9 queries) for every family that passed G4 — a\n     \"win\" that no deployment volume can ever justify.\n  **Default on kill:** the competitiveness programme TERMINATES; restarting\n  requires a NEW proposal with new evidence or a new mechanism class — there is\n  no open-ended maintainer fork. Executive verdict (one paragraph)\n    37\t\n    38\tThe measured Programme-2 NL-boundary failure (l3a-parse 47.6% retention / a5-nl 41.6% +\n    39\tS2 dangerous-wrong) was produced by a **verbatim-lexicon, frozen-frame, no-learning\n    40\tfront-end** — the exact architecture class whose paraphrase ceiling the field measured and\n    41\tabandoned in 2014–2018 [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/{l3a-parse,a5-nl}.json;\n    42\tLIT: berant-liang-2014]. The literature's verdict on that failure class is\n    43\t**capability-limited, not fundamental**: fine-tuned small parsers (110M–3B) with\n    44\tgrammar-constrained decoding routinely hold 80–97% exactness on closed grammars far wider\n    45\tthan kot-query/1's seven families [LIT: picard-2021, codes-2024, tecod-2025,\n    46\tbert-intent-slot-2019], and the specific l3a mechanism (paraphrase stratum 0/261) is the\n    47\tsingle best-understood fixable defect in the field. Two residues are NOT\n    48\tcapability-limited and must be engineered around, not parsed through: (i) **role/direction\n    49\tbinding errors** (the a5-nl dangerous class) sit in the compositional-generalization\n    50\tfamily where fine-tuning scale curves are flat-to-negative [LIT: cogs-2020, cfq-2020,\n    51\tqiu-scale-2022] — the mitigation is checked execution plus calibrated abstention, not a\n    52\tbetter parser; (ii) **ambiguity/underspecification** is a property of natural language\n    53\t[LIT: amp-2024, dte-2023] — the correct output is abstention (or clarification), and\n    54\tdetecting should-abstain inputs is itself a measured-hard task [LIT: triagesql-2020].\n    55\tSelective prediction supplies the missing contract: trained calibrators beat raw\n    56\tconfidence under shift [LIT: kamath-2020], and conformal methods give distribution-free\n    57\tbounds of exactly the S2 shape (\"P(wrong-with-answer) ≤ ε at 1−δ\")\n    58\t[LIT: conformal-abstention-2024, conformal-lm-2024, geifman-2017]. Bottom line for\n    59\tP3-D-NLB: a 0.90-retention, S2-fail-closed front-end at small scale is **plausible and\n    60\tliterature-supported on our closed grammar, but only as a parser+abstention SYSTEM whose\n    61\trisk-coverage curve is the measured product** — and any conformal guarantee is only as\n    62\tgood as the phrasing distribution it was calibrated on [EXTRAPOLATION, argued §4/§6].\n    63\t\n    64\t---\n    65\t\n    66\t## 1. The measured failure, restated precisely, and its literature class\n    67\t\n    68\tWhat actually failed [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json + a5-nl.json]:\n    69\t\n    70\t- The a1-hybrid front-end is **deterministic and training-free**: one entity/identifier\n    71\t  gazetteer + one frozen frame rule set, single-label verbatim concept mapper, ~250–267 µs\n    72\t  per query.\n    73\t- l3a-parse (family/world): 47.6% retention; label-verbatim stratum 76.4% but\n    74\t  **paraphrase stratum 0/261 = 0.0%** (no synonym table); stage breakdown frame-miss 228,\n    75\t  mapper-abstain 48, gazetteer-miss 40, frame-ambiguous 41. Failure mode SAFE (mis-parse →\n    76\t  refusal; S2 did not fire).\n    77\t- a5-nl (code): 41.6% retention, frame-miss 468/gazetteer-miss 24 — AND the S2 kill fired:\n    78\t  the frame ROLE_DIR direction table **flips containment orientation** into\n    79\t  real-but-wrong answers with provenance (5.0% ≥ 2% bar).\n    80\t\n    81\tLiterature mapping [STIPULATED — this taxonomy organizes §5]:\n    82\t\n    83\t| our stage | literature name | canonical instruments |\n    84\t|---|---|---|\n    85\t| frame-miss (paraphrase) | lexical/paraphrase gap of rule- and template-parsers | [LIT: berant-liang-2014, spider-syn-2021, drspider-2023] |\n    86\t| gazetteer-miss | schema linking / entity linking | [LIT: nl2sql-survey-2025, spider-syn-2021, genre-2021] |\n    87\t| frame-ambiguous | NL ambiguity / underspecification | [LIT: amp-2024, dte-2023, triagesql-2020] |\n    88\t| ROLE_DIR flip (dangerous) | role/argument-structure (compositional) generalization error | [LIT: cogs-2020, cfq-2020] |\n    89\t| mapper-abstain | out-of-lexicon abstention (correct behaviour) | selective-prediction frame, §4 |\n    90\t\n    91\tDirection-only reading [EXTRAPOLATION: from the MEASURED l3a-parse stage breakdown + LIT: berant-liang-2014 — never a premise; P3-E-NLB-1 is what tests it]: the 0.0% paraphrase stratum is a **floor artifact of the no-learning front-end**, not a measurement of what parsing can do — the same sentence class that kills a verbatim matcher is the *bread-and-butter positive case* of every learned parser since 2014. This is the single most decision-relevant sentence in this review.\n    92\t\n    93\t---\n    94\t\n    95\t## 2. SQ1 — semantic parsing into executable forms at SMALL model scale: state of the art\n    96\t\n    97\t**The proven recipe at 110M–3B is: fine-tuned seq2seq (or intent+slot) parser + grammar-\n    98\tconstrained decoding + execution-aware selection.** Evidence, smallest-first:\n    99\t\n   100\t- **110M (BERT-base), closed intent+slot grammars:** joint intent classification + slot\n   101\t  filling significantly improves sentence-level semantic-frame accuracy on ATIS/Snips\n   102\t  [LIT: bert-intent-slot-2019; the field-standard ~96–97% figures are paper-body,\n   103\t  UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS at abstract level]. kot-query/1's 7 shape-recoverable families +\n   104\t  gazetteer slots are structurally an intent+slot task, NOT open text-to-SQL\n   105\t  [STIPULATED mapping; design-relevant because it sets the difficulty prior].\n   106\t- **220M–3B, open cross-domain SQL:** fine-tuned T5 + PICARD incremental-parse-constrained\n   107\t  decoding = then-SOTA Spider/CoSQL [LIT: picard-2021]. CodeS (1B–15B open models,\n   108\t  SQL-centric incremental pretraining + schema-linking prompt construction + bidirectional\n   109\t  augmentation) = SOTA accuracy AND robustness on Spider/BIRD/Spider-Syn/-Realistic/-DK/\n   110\t  Dr.Spider at much smaller size than GPT-4 pipelines [LIT: codes-2024]. STaR-SQL adds\n   111\t  self-taught reasoning traces: 86.6% EX Spider [LIT: star-sql-2025, KB record,\n   112\t  UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS].\n   113\t- **The closed/recurring-grammar special case (ours):** TeCoD mines NL→SQL templates,\n   114\t  fine-tunes a template selector, and enforces the chosen template by grammar-constrained\n   115\t  decoding: up to +36% execution accuracy over in-context learning and 2.2× lower latency,\n   116\t  ~90% EX on matched BIRD queries [LIT: tecod-2025]. This is the closest published shape\n   117\t  to FK-NLB-3 (closed template family + constrained decode + classifier), and it sits AT\n   118\t  the 0.90 bar on its matched slice.\n   119\t- **Few-shot, data-scarce:** constrained canonical-utterance parsing (map NL into a\n   120\t  controlled English sublanguage under grammar constraints, then deterministically to\n   121\t  logical form) [LIT: shin-2021]; grammar prompting for DSLs (predict a minimal BNF, then\n   122\t  constrain) [LIT: grammar-prompting-2023]. Both trade accuracy for near-zero training\n   123\t  cost — the fallback if NLB must stay training-free.\n   124\t- **Perspective on difficulty:** open cross-domain text-to-SQL is HARD (best 2018 model\n   125\t  12.4% exact on Spider's DB split [LIT: spider-2018]; ChatGPT 40.08% vs human 92.96%\n   126\t  execution accuracy on realistic BIRD databases [LIT: bird-2023]). Our envelope is the\n   127\t  opposite corner: closed grammar, fixed tiny schema, recurring query shapes — the corner\n   128\t  where the literature's numbers are highest [EXTRAPOLATION, direction-only].\n   129\t\n   130\tLOAD-BEARING (for P3-D-NLB sizing): nothing in this record supports 0.90+ retention from\n   131\ta **zero-learning** front-end under held-out phrasing; everything that clears 0.85+ under\n   132\tparaphrase has a learned component (fine-tuned parser, learned selector, or synonym\n   133\tresources) [EXTRAPOLATION over the §2 record + §1's measured floor].\n   134\t\n   135\t## 2b. Accounting skepticism (what \"beat\" means above)\n   136\t\n   137\t- Constrained decoding wins are **compute-matched by construction** (same model, masked\n   138\t  softmax, near-zero overhead at serving [LIT: xgrammar-2024, UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS]) —\n   139\t  the cleanest wins in this review.\n   140\t- Fine-tuned-small > few-shot-giant claims (CodeS, TeCoD, STaR-SQL) are\n   141\t  **inference-matched but training-unmatched**; under the programme's deployment-\n   142\t  efficiency accounting this is admissible ONLY with the tuning compute declared in the\n   143\t  KOT-LIFE ledger [STIPULATED, per programme §2].\n   144\t- Execution-feedback/verifier wins (§3) spend k× sampling; honest comparisons must hold\n   145\t  k fixed vs self-consistency — LEVER does report against sampling baselines\n   146\t  [LIT: lever-2023]; TinyGSM's 81.5% is teacher-data-unmatched (12.3M synthetic problems\n   147\t  from GPT-3.5) [LIT: tinygsm-2023].\n   148\t- BAR-SQL-class RL-trained abstention claims (\"beats GPT-5\") are single-group on a\n   149\t  self-built benchmark [LIT: barsql-2026] — adopt the *shape* (abstention in the reward),\n   150\t  not the numbers.\n   151\t\n   152\t---\n   153\t\n   154\t## 3. SQ2 — NL→DSL / program synthesis WITH execution feedback\n   155\t\n   180\t  programs, NOT *valid-but-wrong* ones. A direction-flipped kot-query executes perfectly\n   181\t  and returns a well-formed wrong answer — exactly the class DTE documents for text-to-SQL\n   182\t  (plausible SQL for ambiguous/unanswerable questions) [LIT: dte-2023] and BIRD's\n   183\t  human-gap quantifies [LIT: bird-2023]. Execution feedback is therefore necessary-cheap\n   184\t  but nowhere near sufficient for the S2 gate [STIPULATED design law for P3-D-PS].\n   185\t\n   186\t---\n   187\t\n   188\t## 4. SQ3+SQ4 — grammar machinery (delta only) and calibrated abstention / selective prediction\n   189\t\n   190\t**Grammar-constrained decoding, 2025–26 delta over the prior review:**\n   191\t- GCD without finetuning substantially outperforms unconstrained LMs and can beat\n   192\t  task-specific fine-tuned models on structured tasks (IE, entity disambiguation)\n   193\t  [LIT: gcd-geng-2023] — constraints are the cheapest accuracy lever where data is scarce.\n   194\t- BUT locally-masked constrained decoding is **biased sampling** (myopic masking distorts\n   195\t  the conditional distribution and can degrade task performance); automata-tensorized\n   196\t  global constraints + tractable probabilistic proposals fix this at modest cost\n   197\t  [LIT: pgcd-2026, gad-2024 (UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS)]. Design rule: constrain to the CLOSED\n   198\t  kot-query grammar with *global* (automaton-complete) masks — our grammar is regular-ish\n   199\t  and tiny, so exact global masking is trivially feasible [EXTRAPOLATION].\n   200\t- **Grammar induction** (the bead's named sub-question): our grammar is known and closed,\n   201\t  so induction is NOT needed for the target side. Its two proven parser-relevant uses:\n   202\t  (i) induce a grammar over training pairs → recombine → data augmentation that transfers\n   203\t  compositional robustness into a fine-tuned T5 (SOTA on comp-gen + natural-variation\n   204\t  benchmarks) [LIT: csl-2022]; (ii) few-shot explicit rule-system synthesis beats neural\n   205\t  meta-learning when the mapping is genuinely rule-like [LIT: nye-2020]. Use (i) to\n   206\t  manufacture paraphrase training data for NLB [STIPULATED recommendation].\n   207\t\n   208\t**Selective prediction — the frame the NLB gate instrument should adopt:**\n   209\t- Foundations: the risk-coverage trade-off and near-optimal reject strategies\n   210\t  [LIT: elyaniv-wiener-2010]; risk-CONTROLLED selection — set desired risk, reject to\n   211\t  guarantee it w.h.p. (2% top-5 ImageNet error at ~60% coverage) [LIT: geifman-2017].\n   212\t  The NLB gate (\"retention ≥0.90 with S2 ≤2% dangerous-wrong\") is a point on a\n   213\t  risk-coverage curve; the literature-standard instrument reports the whole curve + AUACC\n   214\t  and then FREEZES an operating threshold [STIPULATED adoption].\n   215\t- Raw model confidence is not enough under shift: a trained calibrator answers 56% of\n   216\t  questions at 80% accuracy vs 48% for softmax under domain shift [LIT: kamath-2020].\n   217\t  Parser-specific: calibration of semantic parsers varies by model and dataset; metrics\n   218\t  library + confidence-based challenge splits exist [LIT: calibrated-interpretation-2023].\n   219\t- Selective prediction is trainable into small hosts (PEFT + self-evaluation lifts\n   220\t  AUACC/AUROC) [LIT: aspire-2023]; 2026 variants train abstention directly into the\n   221\t  parser's RL reward (BAR-SQL) [LIT: barsql-2026] or extract it from two-model\n   222\t  deliberation — which **collapses when the verifier is weak/out-of-domain**\n   223\t  [LIT: pvd-2026, caution for small pairs].\n   224\t- **Conformal methods give the S2-shaped guarantee:** conformal abstention bounds the\n   225\t  error (hallucination) rate with rigorous guarantees while abstaining less than log-prob\n   226\t  baselines [LIT: conformal-abstention-2024]; conformal language modeling returns\n   227\t  calibrated generation sets with coverage guarantees [LIT: conformal-lm-2024]; survey of\n   228\t  scope + caveats [LIT: conformal-nlp-survey-2024].\n   229\t- LOAD-BEARING caveat: every conformal guarantee assumes exchangeability with the\n   230\t  calibration set — a paraphrase-distribution shift (exactly what K=1 agent-authored\n   231\t  phrasings vs real users is) silently voids the bound [LIT: conformal-nlp-survey-2024 +\n   232\t  kamath-2020's measured shift degradation; EXTRAPOLATION to our setting]. The NLB gate\n   233\t  must therefore calibrate and evaluate on *disjoint phrasing sources* and stress the\n   234\t  threshold under Dr.Spider-style perturbation [STIPULATED requirement for P3-D-NLB].\n   235\t- Detecting should-abstain inputs is itself hard: cross-domain answerable-vs-unanswerable\n   236\t  classification sits at 60% F1 for RoBERTa [LIT: triagesql-2020]; ambiguity taxonomies +\n   237\t  counterfactual training data help [LIT: dte-2023]; models only represent meaning\n   238\t  distributions of ambiguous inputs when ambiguity is attested in training/prompt\n   239\t  [LIT: amp-2024].\n   240\t\n   241\t---\n   242\t\n   243\t## 5. SQ5 — WHY NL→formal fails: capability-limited vs fundamental, per failure class\n   244\t\n   245\tThe bead's central question, judged per class (method: what moved the number, at what\n   246\tscale, under what accounting):\n   247\t\n   248\t**(a) Lexical/paraphrase gap — CAPABILITY-LIMITED (method-limited), high confidence.**\n   249\tVerbatim/rule front-ends have a hard paraphrase ceiling; the field's fix was learned\n   250\tparaphrase absorption: canonical utterances + paraphrase models [LIT: berant-liang-2014],\n   251\tthen fine-tuned seq2seq, then constrained few-shot canonical parsing [LIT: shin-2021].\n   252\tResidual truth: even LEARNED parsers drop dramatically when explicit NL↔schema lexical\n   253\tcorrespondence is removed (Spider-Syn), and schema synonym annotations recover more than\n   254\tadversarial training [LIT: spider-syn-2021]; the most robust model still loses 14.0%\n   255\toverall / 50.7% worst-perturbation on Dr.Spider [LIT: drspider-2023]. So: fixable to\n   256\t~0.85–0.95 on a closed grammar, NOT to 1.0, and only with paraphrase-diverse training +\n   257\tlexicon resources. l3a-parse's 0/261 says nothing beyond \"no-learning fails\"\n   258\t[EXTRAPOLATION, §1].\n   259\t\n   260\t**(b) Entity/schema linking (gazetteer-miss) — CAPABILITY-LIMITED.** Persistent named\n   261\tcore difficulty of NL2SQL [LIT: nl2sql-survey-2025], but on a FIXED tiny schema with a\n   262\tregistered lexicon, trie-constrained name emission + exact post-mapping (GENRE shape,\n   263\tprior review idea A3) plus synonym annotation [LIT: spider-syn-2021] is the measured\n   264\tstrong recipe [LIT: genre-2021, UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS].\n   265\t\n   266\t**(c) Role/direction binding (the a5-nl dangerous class) — FUNDAMENTAL AT THE\n   267\tPARSER-METHOD LEVEL, manageable at the SYSTEM level.** Structural/argument-role\n   268\tgeneralization is the canonical seq2seq failure: 96–99% in-distribution vs 16–35%\n   269\tcompositional split [LIT: cogs-2020]; accuracy collapses as compound divergence grows\n   270\t[LIT: cfq-2020]; and fine-tuning scale curves on OOD comp-gen are FLAT OR NEGATIVE up to\n   271\t11B (ICL scales but stays below small fine-tuned models) [LIT: qiu-scale-2022]. What has\n   272\tactually moved it: grammar-based data augmentation (CSL) [LIT: csl-2022], explicit rule\n   273\tsynthesis at tiny scale [LIT: nye-2020], and — at the system level — checked execution +\n   274\tverification + abstention (§3, §4), since a direction-flipped query is valid-and-wrong\n   275\tand no output grammar catches it. DESIGN LAW for NLB: role/direction correctness must be\n   276\tadversarially and symmetrically tested (both orientations of every directional frame,\n   277\theld-out compositions), and low-margin directional parses must abstain\n   278\t[STIPULATED, from (c)+§4].\n   279\t\n   280\t**(d) Ambiguity/underspecification — FUNDAMENTAL (property of NL, not of models).**\n   281\tAmbiguous inputs have meaning DISTRIBUTIONS; models capture them only when deliberately\n   282\tinstructed/attested [LIT: amp-2024]; real question streams contain unanswerable and\n   283\tambiguous classes that parsers happily \"answer\" [LIT: dte-2023, triagesql-2020]. No\n   284\tscale fixes this; the correct product behaviour is calibrated abstention (or\n   285\tclarification), which is why the abstention policy is PART of the measured NLB system\n   286\t[STIPULATED, consistent with programme §3.4 H-PS definition].\n   287\t\n   288\t**(e) Confident-wrong-with-provenance (S2) — a SYSTEM failure, prevented by risk-controlled\n   289\tselection, never by parser accuracy alone.** The literature's parsers exhibit it endemically\n   290\t(plausible SQL for bad questions [LIT: dte-2023]; 40% vs 93% human on BIRD with fluent\n   291\toutputs [LIT: bird-2023]); the measured countermeasures are trained calibrators under\n   292\tshift [LIT: kamath-2020], conformal abstention with error-rate bounds\n   293\t[LIT: conformal-abstention-2024], and abstention-aware training rewards [LIT: barsql-2026].\n   294\tThe a5-nl lesson (\"wrong-with-provenance is worse than refusal\") is the field's selective-\n   295\tprediction motivation, verbatim [EXTRAPOLATION, direction-only].\n   296\t\n   297\t**Net judgement for FK-NLB-3:** the measured 47.6%/41.6% failure is capability-limited at\n   298\tour scope — driven ~entirely by classes (a)+(b), which are the two the literature fixes\n   299\tmost reliably — PROVIDED the redesign is a learned-parser+abstention system; classes\n   300\t(c)–(e) are why the redesign must keep the deterministic engine as the sole answer\n   301\tauthority and treat the parser as an untrusted proposer under a risk-controlled gate\n   302\t[EXTRAPOLATION over §5; the single sentence P3-D-NLB should be built around].\n   303\t\n   304\t---\n   305\t\n   306\t## 6. SQ6 — paraphrase robustness: methods that measured\n   307\t\n   308\t1. **Paraphrase-diverse supervision** — the dominant fix: augmentation via induced-grammar\n   309\t   recombination (CSL, SOTA incl. natural-variation splits) [LIT: csl-2022]; bidirectional\n   310\t   augmentation inside CodeS's robustness wins [LIT: codes-2024].\n   311\t2. **Canonical-utterance factoring** — parse NL→canonical utterance→(deterministic) form;\n   312\t   absorbs variation in the NL-NL half where LMs are strongest [LIT: berant-liang-2014,\n   313\t   shin-2021].\n   314\t3. **Lexicon/synonym resources at the interface** — schema synonym annotations beat\n   315\t   adversarial training [LIT: spider-syn-2021]; registered-lexicon trie constraints\n   316\t   [LIT: genre-2021, UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS].\n   317\t4. **Constrained decoding** — removes the syntax/validity failure axis entirely at ~zero\n   318\t   cost, with the global-mask correctness caveat [§4; LIT: picard-2021, pgcd-2026].\n   319\t5. **Evaluation practice (adopt):** robustness is measured with multi-perturbation\n   320\t   diagnostic suites (17 perturbation sets; report per-perturbation worst-case, not just\n   321\t   aggregate) [LIT: drspider-2023]. Our l3a-parse/a5-nl used K=1 held-out phrasing per\n   322\t   query [MEASURED: envelope verbatim] — BELOW field practice; the NLB gate instrument\n   323\t   needs K>1 phrasings per query from an author source disjoint from calibration, plus a\n   324\t   perturbation taxonomy (synonym, structure, typo/ASR-style noise) [STIPULATED\n   325\t   requirement for P3-D-NLB].\n   326\t\n   327\t---\n   328\t\n   329\t## 7. Open questions Phase-1 must resolve (the design beads' opening questions)\n   330\t\n   331\t1. **Learned parser vs fail-closed contract:** a learned proposer introduces a NEW\n   332\t   confident-wrong channel exactly where the deterministic mapper abstained. What\n   333\t   operating threshold on the risk-coverage curve clears BOTH 0.90 retention and S2 ≤2%,\n   334\t   and does any exist for a ≤360M parser on our verticals? (Empirical; nothing in the\n   335\t   literature reports retention-at-S2-bound for closed grammars at this scale.)\n   336\t2. **Guarantee validity under phrasing shift:** conformal/calibration guarantees assume\n   337\t   exchangeability; the deployment phrasing distribution is unknown. Split-source\n   338\t   calibration, shift stress tests, and (possibly) online recalibration need a design —\n   339\t   the literature flags the problem more than it solves it [LIT: conformal-nlp-survey-2024,\n   340\t   kamath-2020].\n   341\t3. **Cost envelope:** the a1-hybrid ran at ~250 µs; a 135–360M parser is ~ms–10s-of-ms on\n   342\t   our 2 shared CPU cores. Does NLB admit a two-tier front-end (µs template/trie fast path\n   343\t   [LIT: tecod-2025] + ms learned fallback + abstain), and what does that do to the\n   344\t   KOT-COST vector? (Design choice, no literature blocker.)\n   345\t4. **Direction/role adversarial coverage:** how to enumerate and symmetrically test every\n   346\t   directional frame (the a5-nl ROLE_DIR class) — needs a per-vertical inventory and a\n   347\t   both-orientations eval design [§5(c)].\n   348\t5. **Composition with the verifier loop:** f2b's verify-retry and NLB's parse-abstention\n   349\t   are both selective mechanisms; are their failures correlated (same host, same phrasing\n   350\t   sensitivity)? Unmeasured anywhere [EXTRAPOLATION cell].\n   351\t6. **Generation-from-checked-result:** H-PS's last leg (surface answer FROM the engine\n   352\t   result) is cautioned by nsk1-g2d's net-harmful text append [MEASURED]; TinyGSM's\n   353\t   numeric answers dodge this. Spawns (recommendations only — the coordinator creates beads):\n> **P3-D-INDEX, P3-D-SEAL, P3-D-POWER, P3-D-BASE** (§9).\n>\n> **Tag convention** (mapping the house discipline onto a lit-review):\n> `[LIT-BACKED: source, year]` = external empirical fact verified AT SOURCE this\n> session (WebFetch/WebSearch on 2026-07-11; URL in §10 / EVAL.sources.jsonl);\n> `[MEASURED: registry/...]` = restatement of a programme verdict inside its own\n> envelope; `[STIPULATED]` = a judgement or recommendation I make here;\n> `[EXTRAPOLATION]` = a forward projection, never a premise. Anything not\n> verifiable at source is marked **UNVERIFIED** inline, never silently carried.\n\n---\n\n## 0. Dedup: what already exists in-repo, and what this review adds\n\nSurveyed before anything was added (the DEDUP-FIRST instruction):\n\n- `reports/lit-eval-benchmarks.md` (Kern, 2026-07-10) — a G3-freeze fact sheet\n  verifying at source: CLUTRR (arXiv:1908.06177, EMNLP 2019, CC-BY-NC-4.0,\n  generator-based), the nine SmolLM2-card benchmarks (papers, licences, sizes,\n  metrics, card scores incl. the MMLU cloze-vs-MMLU-Pro card inconsistency),\n  HumanEval/MBPP/SWE-bench lineage, and the SWE-bench-Verified contamination\n  deprecation (OpenAI ~2026-02-23: gold-patch reproduction + 59.4%-flawed-test\n  audit; SWE-bench Pro's copyleft+held-out design as the replacement). **This\n  review does not re-derive any of those facts; it cites them.**\n- `docs/next/benchmark-evaluation-strategy.md` (2026-07-10) — the SmolLM2-suite\n  triage (covered-help / non-regression / math-route), the anchoring protocol\n  (±2.0 pp harness-fidelity tolerance), the Δ_B = κ_B × Δ_covered identity, and\n  the \"no cross-benchmark aggregation over the nine\" rule. Programme-3 rev-2\n  already demoted that nine-task suite to KOT-SMOL-CONT/1 (ASM-0811); this\n  review supplies what replaces it.\n- `kb/records/` (639 records, kot-lit-1 schema) — **architecture/mechanism\n  records only** (seams, steering, PRMs, constrained decoding, knowledge\n  injection). There is NO eval-methodology shard; the closest matches (Logic-LM,\n  VersaPRM, MASPRM, draft-conditioned CD) are baseline-component prior art for\n  P3-LR-RAG/P3-LR-RULE, not index methodology. Nothing below duplicates a KB\n  record. Per governance, nothing here is ingested into the KB — the staging\n  file `docs/next/lit/EVAL.sources.jsonl` is for the coordinator's central\n  ingest.\n- `docs/next/eval-necessity-adjudication.md`, `docs/research-plan/08-stats-*` —\n  provenance-triple + one-primary discipline, inherited not restated.\n\nWhat is NEW here: HELM's construction and aggregation; the OLLv2 component set,\nnormalization mathematics, and measured tiny-scale floors; contamination\ndetection limits + how sealed benchmarks are actually produced; proxy-rung\nvalidity (the metric-dependence result); per-benchmark floor/ceiling evidence\nfor the KOT-AI-INDEX/2 D1–D7 table; and the four Phase-1 design hand-offs.\n\n---\n\n## 1. HELM: multi-scenario construction, and why a flat accuracy average is inadequate\n\n- LOAD-BEARING: HELM (Liang, Bommasani, Lee et al., \"Holistic Evaluation of\n  Language Models\", TMLR 2023) evaluates 42 scenarios (16 core + 26 targeted)\n  under 7 metrics per core scenario — accuracy, calibration, robustness,\n  fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency — with standardized conditions that\n  raised cross-model scenario coverage from 17.9% to 96.0% over 30 models\n  [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2211.09110, TMLR 2023].\n- The construction the review told us to copy is the two-level structure:\n  scenarios (use cases) × metrics (desiderata), taxonomized first and THEN\n  populated, so gaps are explicit rather than invisible. A flat accuracy\n  average has no seat for calibration/robustness at all, and mixes scenarios\n  with different chance floors and variances into one number — HELM's central\n  argument is that single-metric evaluation lets \"metrics beyond accuracy fall\n  to the wayside\" and hides trade-offs [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2211.09110, 2023].\n- Aggregation caution: HELM's original headline aggregate was the **mean win\n  rate** (fraction of head-to-head comparisons won across scenarios). This is\n  ORDINAL — it obscures effect magnitude, and it is comparator-set-dependent:\n  adding/removing models changes every score, and small scenario-score\n  variations can invert ranks. Later HELM-family work moved to mean score over\n  normalized metrics for exactly this reason. **UNVERIFIED at primary source**\n  for the internal aggregation details (read via secondary sources — the AHELM\n  paper and topic surveys; the HELM paper abstract itself was fetched but its\n  aggregation section was not) — carried as a flag, not a fact.\n- DECISION for P3-D-INDEX [STIPULATED]: adopt HELM's *structure* (domains ×\n  metrics, gaps explicit, vector always published) but NOT mean-win-rate\n  aggregation — a win-rate scalar would make KOT-AI-INDEX/2 comparator-set\n  dependent, which directly conflicts with W1's pre-registered frontier F(B_k)\n  (adding a comparator must not move S's own index). Use chance/ceiling\n  normalization + macro-averaging (§2), which is absolute per system.\n\n## 2. Composite-index statistics: normalization, macro-averaging, and when a scalar is defensible\n\n**Normalization against chance (the practice standard).**\n\n- LOAD-BEARING: the Open LLM Leaderboard v2 normalizes every score by\n  subtracting the task's random-guess baseline and rescaling to 0–100:\n  `norm = (raw − lower_bound)/(1 − lower_bound)`, clamped to 0 below chance;\n  for tasks with heterogeneous subtasks (BBH's 24 subtasks with num_choices\n  2–19; MuSR's 2/5/3-choice subtasks) each subtask is normalized against ITS\n  OWN chance floor first and the normalized scores are then averaged;\n  generative tasks (MATH, IFEval) take lower_bound = 0 [LIT-BACKED:\n  huggingface.co/docs/leaderboards/open_llm_leaderboard/normalization, 2024].\n- This verifies the exact shape ASM-0811 step (1)–(2) stipulated: normalize\n  within-benchmark against chance, THEN average — with one difference:\n  **OLLv2 pins the ceiling at 1.0; KOT-AI-INDEX/2 additionally pins a\n  benchmark-specific ceiling** (human/large-reference). No published leaderboard\n  we verified does ceiling-pinning; that piece is ours to freeze and defend\n  [STIPULATED]. The candidate ceilings this review verified: BLiMP human 88.6%\n  (individual) / 96.4% (aggregate) [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:1912.00582, TACL 2020];\n  EWoK human > all tested models 1.3B–70B (exact human % in paper body, not\n  re-verified here — pin at freeze) [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2405.09605, TACL 2025].\n  Where no human ceiling exists, ASM-0811's \"named pinned reference model\"\n  fallback stands.\n- Different answer cardinalities are therefore handled (per-subtask floors),\n  but **different variances are NOT handled by OLLv2** — a 250-item benchmark\n  and a 14k-item benchmark enter the average with equal weight and very\n  different standard errors. The §2.5 hierarchical bootstrap (families → items,\n  paired) is the correct treatment and has no off-the-shelf leaderboard\n  precedent we could verify; it must be specified in the P3-D-INDEX analysis\n  plan [STIPULATED].\n\n**Is a scalar defensible at all?**\n\n- LOAD-BEARING: metabench (Kipnis et al., ICLR 2025) fit IRT models over\n  ~5,000 public LLMs × 28,632 items from six benchmarks (ARC, GSM8K, HellaSwag,\n  MMLU, TruthfulQA, WinoGrande) and found (a) <3% of items reconstruct each\n  benchmark score at 1.24% RMSE and the total at 0.58% RMSE, and (b) a single\n  common factor with Spearman r = 0.94 against the total score [LIT-BACKED:\n  arXiv:2407.12844, ICLR 2025].\n- Reading for us [STIPULATED]: the six legacy benchmarks are so redundant that\n  their average measures ONE factor — which is precisely the GPT-5.6 objection\n  to revision-1's nine-task suite (seven correlated MC tasks). A scalar over a\n  suite dominated by one factor is defensible but UNINFORMATIVE about a\n  neurosymbolic system whose hypothesis is a *profile* change (D4 up, D2 flat).\n  This is independent quantitative support for the domain-vector-first rule:\n  the scalar exists for ranking, the vector for honesty (ASM-0811 (3)), and for\n  the D4 diagnostics being non-optional — they are the only components not\n  loading on the legacy common factor. It also licenses item-level suite\n  compression at R-1 (IRT-selected subsets) as a cost lever IF we accept\n  reconstruction error as added variance — flagged as a P3-D-INDEX option, not\n  a default [EXTRAPOLATION].\n\n## 3. Open-LLM-Leaderboard-v2: components, and which floor out at 100M–2B\n\n- LOAD-BEARING: OLLv2's six components and setups are: IFEval (0-shot, strict\n  acc, prompt+inst level), BBH (3-shot, acc_norm over 24 subtasks, choices\n  2–19), MATH Lvl-5 (4-shot, exact match), GPQA (0-shot, acc_norm, 4-choice),\n  MuSR (0-shot, acc_norm, 3 subtasks: 2/5/3-choice), MMLU-Pro (5-shot, acc,\n  10-choice); run on the EleutherAI lm-evaluation-harness with chat template\n  for instruct models [LIT-BACKED:\n  huggingface.co/docs/leaderboards/open_llm_leaderboard/about, 2024].\n- Floor evidence at the programme's rungs, from the SmolLM2 instruct cards\n  (own-harness lighteval; anchor numbers, not our measurements):\n  - MMLU-Pro MCF: 1.7B-Instruct **19.3** vs 10% chance (10-choice) — ~9 pp of\n    normalized headroom; Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct 24.2, Llama-1B-Instruct 12.7\n    (≈ 3 pp above chance) [LIT-BACKED: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct\n    card, 2025]. At 135M/360M the cards do not even report MMLU-Pro (they\n    report MMLU-cloze) — the fact-sheet inconsistency stands [LIT-BACKED:\n    reports/lit-eval-benchmarks.md §2 + HuggingFaceTB cards, 2025].\n  - BBH 3-shot: 1.7B-Instruct **32.2**, 135M-Instruct **28.2** [LIT-BACKED:\n    SmolLM2 instruct cards, 2025]. Since many BBH subtasks are 2–3 choice\n    (raw chance on the suite is roughly 25–30% region depending on mix), a\n    28–32 raw score is at-or-near the per-subtask-normalized FLOOR — the 135M\n    vs 1.7B gap of 4 raw points confirms BBH barely discriminates ≤2B\n    [EXTRAPOLATION from the verified num_choices list; exact normalized floor\n    to be computed mechanically in P3-D-INDEX from the pinned subtask list].\n  - GSM8K: base cards 1.4 / 3.2 / 31.0 (135M/360M/1.7B, 5-shot); 48.2 at\n    1.7B-Instruct [LIT-BACKED: reports/lit-eval-benchmarks.md §2.9 +\n    SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct card, 2025]. Floored at ≤360M; discriminative at\n    1.7B → D5 enters at R-2 (≥1.7B-class), not R-1, unless the R-1 rung is\n    ≥1B [STIPULATED].\n  - IFEval: 135M-Instruct **29.9** (card's prompt/inst average), 1.7B-Instruct\n    **56.7** [LIT-BACKED: SmolLM2 instruct cards, 2025]. IFEval's floor is ~0\n    (generative, strict verification), so it is above floor and discriminative\n    ACROSS the whole 135M–1.7B range — better than the review's \"iff above\n    floor at R-2\" prior suggested. Contamination: what detection can and cannot do, and how sealed benchmarks are produced\n\n**Detection limits (the review's skepticism is literature-confirmed).**\n\n- LOAD-BEARING: simple variations of test data — paraphrase, translation —\n  defeat n-gram/string-match decontamination: a 13B model trained on rephrased\n  test sets reached GPT-4-level scores on MMLU/GSM8K/HumanEval while passing\n  standard decontamination; their stronger LLM-based decontaminator then found\n  8–18% of HumanEval overlapping in RedPajama-1T/StarCoder-Data, and\n  contamination even inside GPT-3.5/4-generated synthetic data [LIT-BACKED:\n  Yang, Chiang, Zheng, Gonzalez, Stoica, arXiv:2311.04850, 2023].\n- LOAD-BEARING: contamination can be PROVEN (not just screened) from black-box\n  log-probabilities via exchangeability — a contaminated model assigns higher\n  likelihood to the benchmark's canonical ordering than to shuffles; works down\n  to 1.4B models, 1,000-example sets, few duplications — but requires logprob\n  access and duplication-rate knowledge, and a NEGATIVE result proves nothing\n  [LIT-BACKED: Oren, Meister, Chatterji, Ladhak, Hashimoto, arXiv:2310.17623,\n  2023 (ICLR 2024 — venue not restated on the arXiv page; UNVERIFIED as venue)].\n- Design consequence [STIPULATED]: the §2.2 screener (n-gram + embedding) is a\n  gross-leakage gate ONLY — the literature says paraphrase-level leakage passes\n  it, which is exactly why the rev-2 design made the sealed evaluation (§2.2a)\n  mandatory rather than optional. For OUR stores (which we control) the Oren\n  test is inapplicable (it detects training-data memorization); the screener +\n  authoring provenance rules carry that side. For DONOR WEIGHTS, the Oren test\n  is a cheap, runnable diagnostic on any open comparator with logprob access —\n  add it to the P3-D-SEAL toolbox.\n\n**Producing a sealed/refreshed held-out benchmark (three verified recipes).**\n\n- LOAD-BEARING (parallel-replica recipe): GSM1k built 1,000 NEW human-written\n  problems matched to GSM8K on human solve rates, solution step counts, and\n  answer magnitudes, kept the set private, and measured overfitting as the\n  GSM8k→GSM1k gap: drops up to 8%, systematic across some model families,\n  minimal at the frontier, with the gap correlating (Spearman r² = 0.36) with\n  the model's probability of *generating* GSM8k items verbatim [LIT-BACKED:\n  Zhang et al., arXiv:2405.00332, NeurIPS 2024 D&B].\n- LOAD-BEARING (refresh recipe): LiveBench adds/updates questions monthly from\n  fresh sources (competitions, arXiv, news), scores against objective ground\n  truth without LLM judges, and versions each release; ICLR 2025 Spotlight\n  [LIT-BACKED: White et al., arXiv:2406.19314, ICLR 2025]. What Programme-3 is, and what it inherits\n\nThe maintainer's intent, restated: use something *like* the kernel + world\ninformation this programme has built to create **neurosymbolic AI architectures**\nthat (1) achieve a **higher AI index than comparably-resourced models**, and then\n(2) find how **small** the whole system (model + kernel + world + everything else)\ncan be made while staying competitive on that index. Because the system is\nneurosymbolic, size cannot be parameters: size is **deployment bytes** plus a full\n**inference resource vector**. Start very small to learn cheaply which\narchitectures work; then continuously scale while improving them. (The review is\nright that the literal \"higher than ALL other models of the same size and compute\"\nis not a testable claim; §1.4 narrows what W1 actually licenses — the maintainer's\nnorth-star is preserved as the *aspiration*, the testable claim is the frontier\nstatement.)\n\nProgramme-3 is a **new goal**, not a new evidence base. It inherits Programme-1/2's\ninstruments, seams, and — critically — its measured walls. The honest starting\nposition, restated from the capstone with its own tags:\n\n- PREMISE: across the entire registry there are ZERO audited end-task wins over the\n  kernel-as-text null attributable to the kernel's content; registry/assessments/b-cov-define-lane.json].\n- PREMISE: model-internal grounding is unresolved two-sided: text-delivered\n  grounding measured NET-HARMFUL (0.76 → 0.43 on append; 0/24 rescues), the\n  residual-stream channel delivers at echo grade (keyacc 0.81/0.85) but integration\n  into free generation is unresolved (R− rescue 0/8) [MEASURED:\n  registry/assessments/nsk1-g2d.json + nsk1-bprime2.json, exploratory/DRAFT status].\n- PREMISE: cross-lingual concept minting shows a large MEMBERSHIP UPPER BOUND on\n  prefill savings (~18.5–24.0% Qwen2.5 / 33.4–41.7% SmolLM2 at the 10k-concept\n  anchor) with the entire consumption channel and mint-cost side unmeasured\n  [MEASURED: registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json, Tier-0 exploratory census — an\n  upper bound, never achievable savings].\n\nThree consequences shape everything below.\n\n- DECISION: Programme-3 adopts the four inherited hard constraints — (1) no\n  real-input claim without an FK-NLB-3-class NL front-end gate cleared per vertical;\n  (2) every kernel-contribution claim carries deranged-store + aligned-non-kernel\n  controls;
